bead.title: agent: add /proc fd-based worktree attribution fallback for orphan processes with unreadable cwd
bead.description:
PROBLEM
procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:57-74) drops a process entirely -- it is never even offered to classification -- whenever os.Readlink("/proc/<pid>/cwd") fails or returns empty (execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:63,70-72), or when the resolved cwd no longer maps to any known root. Such a process is invisible to the leftover-worktree scan even though it may still hold open files inside a leased worktree (design: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-040-leftover-worktree-process-reaping.md, "Change 2").

ROOT CAUSE
Worktree attribution today has exactly one signal: cwd (executionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus, execution_cleanup_process.go:415-437, fed from procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect at execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:63). If proc.Worktree ends up empty, inspect returns `(executionCleanupAttemptProcess{}, false)` at execution_cleanup_process_linux.go:70-72 and the process is dropped from the scan entirely -- it never reaches classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup, so it can never be reaped no matter how long it has been orphaned.

PROPOSED FIX
In procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect, when cwd-based attribution yields an empty Worktree, add a fallback: enumerate /proc/<pid>/fd/*, resolve each entry's symlink target (best-effort, skipping unreadable descriptors), and apply the same root-truncation rule used for cwd attribution (reuse or extract the truncate-to-root helper introduced or existing in execution_cleanup_process.go) against each resolved target. The first fd target that truncates to a root under the configured tempRoot becomes Worktree. This is strictly additive: cwd-based attribution keeps priority, and a process with no cwd signal and no matching fd signal remains unclassified (excluded from the scan, never killed), preserving today's conservative default.

NON-SCOPE
- No change to the classification/kill-safety logic in classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup -- this bead only widens the detection signal feeding it.
- No non-Linux implementation -- mirrors the existing execution_cleanup_process_other.go no-op split.
- No change to the cwd-based root-normalization fix (separate child bead, ddx-79dab565).
bead.acceptance:
1. procExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner.inspect (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_linux.go) falls back to scanning /proc/<pid>/fd/* symlink targets for worktree attribution when cwd-based attribution resolves to an empty Worktree.
2. New test TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner_FdFallbackAttribution in a `_linux_test.go` real-process test file (following the spawn-and-inspect pattern of orphan_harness_reaper_realproc_linux_test.go) spawns a helper process with cwd outside every known root but an open file descriptor pointing under a fake worktree root, and asserts the scanner attributes proc.Worktree to that root.
3. New test TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner_NoFallbackWhenNoRootedFd (or equivalent) asserts a process with neither a rooted cwd nor a rooted open fd remains excluded from the scan (Scan omits it), unchanged from current behavior.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestProcExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessScanner green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
